intro: Oreoseris maxima, commonly known as the Himalayan or Guggul tree, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of Oreoseris maxima include its potential to reduce cholesterol levels, improve blood sugar control, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable remedy for cardiovascular and metabolic health issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include guggulsterones, which are steroid molecules responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. Oreoseris maxima is typically made into herbal preparations such as guggul resin, guggul powder, and guggul oil, which are used in Ayurvedic medicine to support overall health and well-being.
